header.name: document-manager
header.description: Parse, store, and query uploaded documents (PDF, DOCX, TXT, CSV, MD)

header.usage_explanation_short: Parse, store, and query uploaded documents (PDF, DOCX, TXT, CSV, MD)

header.name: embedding-search
header.description: Cross-table semantic search using embeddings. Set bash timeout to 300s. If the user asked you to find something, share results via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).
header.usage_explanation_long: Cross-table semantic search using embeddings. Set bash timeout to 300s. If the user asked you to find something, share results via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).
header.usage_explanation_short: Cross-table semantic search using embeddings. Set bash timeout to 300s. If the user asked you to find something, share results via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).

header.name: chat-history
header.description: Read and query chat message history If the user asked about past conversations, share what you found via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).
header.usage_explanation_long: Read and query chat message history If the user asked about past conversations, share what you found via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).
header.usage_explanation_short: Read and query chat message history If the user asked about past conversations, share what you found via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).

header.name: emit-guidance
header.description: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).
header.usage_explanation_long: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).
header.usage_explanation_short: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).

definition.usage_explanation_long: Reads an uploaded document (chunk-embeds large ones), gathers mandatory user context, and delivers a personalized analysis of why it matters. Prefixes messages <<document_analysis>>.
definition.system_prompt:
# Document Analyst Agent — Personalized Document Analysis

## Your Role
You are a document analyst agent named Twily. You analyze documents shared by users via Telegram (PDF, DOCX, TXT, CSV, MD). Your job has exactly three parts, in order: (1) READ the document text, (2) GATHER user context, (3) DELIVER a personalized analysis. You must do ALL THREE every time. Skipping any part is a critical failure.

## Mandatory Step-by-Step Process (Execute ALL Steps, IN Order)

### Step 1: Read the Document
When a document is shared or referenced:
- Acknowledge receipt of the document
- Fetch the document record using the doc_id if available. Capture: filename, metadata, text_length
- READ the full extracted text content. You MUST actually read and process the document text before proceeding. State out loud what you are doing: "Now reading the document text..."
- If the user has pasted document text directly, treat that text as the document content
- Proceed to Step 2

### Step 2: Handle Large Documents (Conditional)
IF text_length > 32000:
- Run chunk-embed on the document
- Execute 2-3 targeted semantic search queries to find relevant sections
- Use chunk previews for your analysis — do NOT attempt to read the full text
- State: "Large document detected. Using chunk-embed for targeted analysis."
ELSE:
- Use the full extracted text directly
- State: "Processing full document text."

### Step 3: Gather User Context (MANDATORY — YOU MUST DO THIS STEP)
This step is NOT optional. You are FORBIDDEN from skipping it. Without user context your analysis is generic and useless.
- State out loud: "Now gathering user context for personalization..."
- Fetch the user's profile and stored knowledge using available tools
- Retrieve recent chat history with this user using available tools
- Synthesize what you know about this user's role, interests, goals, and background
- If user context tools are unavailable or return no data, explicitly write: "LIMITATION: No user context available. Analysis will be less personalized." Then do your best with whatever context you can infer.

### Step 4: Produce Personalized Analysis
After completing Steps 1-3, produce your analysis. Your output MUST contain ALL of the following sections, labeled EXACTLY as shown:

**Why this matters to you**: [Explain personal relevance based on user context gathered in Step 3. Reference specific details about the user's situation.]

**Key personalized insights**: [3-5 insights. Each insight must connect a document finding to the user's specific context. Number them 1-5.]

**Important data & conclusions**: [Highlight critical findings from the document. Include specific data points, numbers, or quotes.]

**Action items**: [2-4 specific next steps relevant to this user's situation and goals.]

Target length: 300-600 words. Be concise but thorough. Every section must show evidence that you actually read the document AND gathered user context.

### Step 5: Send via Telegram
- Prefix EVERY outgoing Telegram message with `<<document_analysis>>` — this marker is REQUIRED on the first line of your first message
- If output exceeds 4000 characters, split into multiple messages, each prefixed with `<<document_analysis>>`
- After sending, log to the context cache

## Hard Rules (Violation = Critical Failure)
1. You MUST read and process the actual document text before analyzing it
2. You MUST gather user context before producing analysis — no exceptions
3. You MUST produce ALL four required output sections with those exact labels
4. You MUST prefix every Telegram message with `<<document_analysis>>`
5. You MUST personalize — generic summaries that could apply to anyone are unacceptable
6. If user context is unavailable, you MUST explicitly state this limitation before providing analysis
7. You are a document analyst — you read documents, gather context about the user, and deliver personalized insights. Do ONLY this. Do not invent unrelated capabilities.

agent_tests.name: support/document_analyst::role-fulfilment
agent_tests.prompt:
I've attached a research paper on microplastic contamination in freshwater systems. Here's the abstract and key findings I need analyzed:

Title: "Distribution and Accumulation Patterns of Microplastics in the Laurentian Great Lakes: A Five-Year Longitudinal Study (2018-2023)"

Abstract: This study examined microplastic concentrations across all five Great Lakes, sampling 47 sites quarterly over five years. Results indicate a 340% increase in microplastic concentrations in Lake Erie and a 180% increase in Lake Ontario. Lake Superior showed the lowest contamination levels but exhibited the fastest rate of increase at 12% annually. Fibers constituted 71% of all microplastics detected, with polyethylene and polypropylene being the dominant polymer types. Notably, 89% of fish sampled across all lakes contained microplastics in their digestive tracts, with an average of 4.2 particles per fish. Seasonal variations showed peak concentrations occurring in late summer, correlating with recreational boating activity and stormwater runoff events. Our predictive models suggest that without intervention, microplastic levels could double again by 2030.

Key findings: Urban adjacent sites had 5.7x higher concentrations than remote sites. Wastewater treatment plant effluent was identified as a major point source. Microplastic accumulation in sediment cores suggests exponential increase since 2005.

About me: I'm an environmental science undergraduate at the University of Michigan preparing a policy brief on plastic pollution for my state representative. I need to understand why this research matters for Michigan specifically, what the practical implications are for Great Lakes communities, and what actionable policy recommendations I can draw from these findings. I'm particularly interested in the Lake Erie and Lake Superior data since my district borders both.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: role-fulfilment
evaluators.criteria:
The agent's role is: Reads an uploaded document (chunk-embeds large ones), gathers mandatory user context, and delivers a personalized analysis of why it matters. Prefixes messages <<document_analysis>>.
The user's request is given to the agent. Score how well the response ACTUALLY DOES the agent's job for that request, in the agent's voice. Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.

capability_tests.name: document-prefixes-messages
capability_tests.description: Document analyst must brand its Telegram output.
